name: birth-hatshepsut

name: birth-akhenaten

name: birth-nefertiti
description: The Egyptian queen and wife of Akhenaten, famed for her painted bust and royal influence.

name: birth-ramesses-ii
description: The Egyptian pharaoh 'Ramesses the Great' famed for his monuments and long, powerful reign.

name: birth-homer
description: The Greek epic poet traditionally credited with composing the Iliad and the Odyssey.

name: birth-sappho
description: The Greek lyric poet of Lesbos celebrated in antiquity for her intimate verses on love.

name: birth-solon
description: The Athenian statesman and lawmaker whose reforms laid the foundations for later democracy.

description: The Chinese sage traditionally credited with the Tao Te Ching and founding philosophical Taoism.

name: birth-pythagoras

name: birth-sun-tzu

name: birth-sophocles

name: birth-herodotus

name: death-leonidas-i

name: death-xerxes-i
description: The Persian king who invaded Greece and fought at Thermopylae was assassinated in his palace.

name: birth-thucydides

name: birth-plato

name: birth-diogenes
description: The Greek founder of Cynic philosophy who lived in a barrel and mocked social convention.

name: birth-aristotle

name: birth-epicurus

name: birth-euclid

name: birth-archimedes

name: death-spartacus

name: birth-hadrian

name: birth-jorge-luis-borges
description: The Argentine writer of labyrinthine short stories that fused philosophy, fantasy, and metafiction.

description: The Italian physicist who built the first nuclear reactor and pioneered the atomic age.

name: birth-werner-heisenberg
description: The German physicist whose uncertainty principle became a cornerstone of quantum mechanics.

name: birth-louis-armstrong
description: The American trumpeter and singer whose virtuosity and charisma made him a defining voice of jazz.

name: birth-walt-disney
description: The American animator who created Mickey Mouse and built an entertainment empire of films and theme parks.

name: birth-charles-lindbergh
description: The American aviator who made the first solo nonstop flight across the Atlantic.

name: birth-john-steinbeck
description: The American author of The Grapes of Wrath, a Nobel laureate who portrayed Depression-era hardship.

description: The English author of Nineteen Eighty-Four and Animal Farm, whose work warned against totalitarianism.

name: birth-john-von-neumann
description: The Hungarian mathematician who pioneered game theory and the architecture of modern computers.

name: birth-robert-oppenheimer
description: The American physicist who led the Manhattan Project to build the first atomic bomb.

name: birth-pablo-neruda
description: The Chilean poet and Nobel laureate celebrated for his passionate love poems and political verse.

name: birth-salvador-dali
description: The Spanish painter of melting clocks whose surrealist images explored dreams and the subconscious.

name: birth-jean-paul-sartre
description: The French philosopher and leading voice of existentialism who declined the Nobel Prize.

name: birth-kurt-godel
description: The Austrian logician whose incompleteness theorems transformed mathematics and logic.

name: birth-dmitri-shostakovich
description: The Russian composer whose symphonies endured an uneasy relationship with the Soviet state.

name: birth-frida-kahlo
description: The Mexican painter whose vivid self-portraits explored identity, pain, and her own body.

name: birth-katharine-hepburn
description: The American actress and four-time Oscar winner known for fierce independence and screen wit.

name: birth-lyndon-johnson
description: The 36th US president who enacted landmark civil rights laws and escalated the Vietnam War.

name: birth-simone-de-beauvoir
description: The French philosopher whose book The Second Sex became a foundational text of feminism.

name: birth-jacques-cousteau
description: The French oceanographer who co-invented the aqua-lung and popularized undersea exploration on film.

name: birth-mother-teresa
description: The Albanian-Indian nun who served Kolkata's poor and was later canonized as a Catholic saint.

name: birth-alan-turing
description: The English mathematician who broke the Enigma code and laid the foundations of computer science.

name: birth-jackson-pollock
description: The American painter who pioneered drip-painting and led the Abstract Expressionist movement.

name: birth-albert-camus
description: The French author of The Stranger and a Nobel laureate associated with the philosophy of the absurd.

name: birth-richard-nixon
description: The 37th US president who opened relations with China and resigned over the Watergate scandal.

name: birth-rosa-parks
description: The American activist whose refusal to give up her bus seat sparked the Montgomery boycott.

name: birth-frank-sinatra
description: The American singer and actor whose smooth voice made him one of the most popular entertainers ever.

name: birth-orson-welles
description: The American filmmaker behind Citizen Kane, widely regarded as one of cinema's greatest works.

name: birth-richard-feynman
description: The American physicist who reshaped quantum electrodynamics and shared the Nobel Prize in Physics.

name: birth-jackie-robinson
description: The American ballplayer who broke baseball's color barrier, becoming a civil rights icon.

name: birth-isaac-asimov
description: The prolific American author of the Foundation and Robot series who shaped modern science fiction.

name: birth-john-paul-ii
friendly_name: Birth of Pope John Paul II
description: The Polish pope who led the Catholic Church for decades and helped hasten communism's fall in Europe.

name: birth-betty-friedan
description: The American writer whose book The Feminine Mystique ignited second-wave feminism in the 1960s.

name: birth-maria-callas
description: The Greek-American soprano whose dramatic voice and artistry revived the bel canto opera tradition.

name: birth-george-hw-bush
friendly_name: Birth of George H. W. Bush
description: The 41st US president who oversaw the Cold War's end and led the Gulf War coalition.

name: birth-margaret-thatcher
description: Britain's first woman prime minister, the 'Iron Lady' who reshaped the economy through the 1980s.

name: birth-fidel-castro
description: The revolutionary who led Cuba's revolution and ruled the communist island for decades.

name: birth-marilyn-monroe
description: The American actress and Hollywood icon famed for Some Like It Hot and her enduring star allure.

name: birth-andy-warhol
description: The American artist who led the Pop Art movement with works like his Campbell's Soup Cans.

name: birth-maya-angelou
description: The American poet and memoirist behind I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings and a civil rights voice.

name: birth-anne-frank
description: The German-Dutch diarist whose journal in hiding became the most-read account of the Holocaust.

name: birth-audrey-hepburn

name: birth-neil-armstrong

name: birth-mikhail-gorbachev

name: birth-elizabeth-taylor
description: The British-American actress and Cleopatra star, a screen legend and pioneering AIDS activist.

name: birth-carl-sagan

name: birth-yuri-gagarin

name: birth-elvis-presley

name: birth-dalai-lama

name: birth-jimi-hendrix
description: The American guitarist who redefined rock with virtuosic, electrifying playing in the 1960s.

name: birth-stephen-hawking

description: The Jamaican musician who brought reggae and Rastafari to the world with songs of unity and peace.

name: death-benito-mussolini

description: The British singer and Queen frontman renowned for his vocal range and electric stage presence.

name: birth-david-bowie
description: The British musician and shape-shifting innovator behind Ziggy Stardust and decades of pop reinvention.

name: death-john-f-kennedy
description: The 35th US president, who led during the Cuban Missile Crisis, was assassinated in Dallas.

name: birth-kobe-bryant
description: The American basketball star who won five NBA titles with the Lakers across a 20-year career.

name: death-saddam-hussein
description: The Iraqi dictator, ousted in a US-led invasion, was executed by hanging in Baghdad.

name: death-nelson-mandela
description: The anti-apartheid leader who, after 27 years in prison, became South Africa's first Black president.
